whatsNew: Dials and presets — change the look without breaking coherence. 'Make it more minimal' is something you can just say; a skill only earns its place where one word must move many tokens at once without breaking a rule. New /ss-dial does exactly that for seven axes (density · hierarchy · radius · elevation · color · weight · motion): a deterministic ramp that shifts the whole token set together, keeps the guardrails (8px grid, ≥44px touch, single accent, nested-radius, ≤8% shadow), clamps at the ends, and re-runs the gate. New /ss-restyle applies a named aesthetic — swiss · editorial · technical · warm-dtc · minimal-mono · brutalist-lite — as a coherent coordinate across those axes plus a font and a signature move, written to the lock (glassmorphism/neumorphism deliberately excluded as dated, §CC-9d). One axis per dial, one preset replaces the look — stacking is the mixed-personality tell we ban.
update: Run /ss-update, or: npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed
